# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-09

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, **and 1 done.** Config templates, recipe schema, seed recipes, and
  gitignore rules are in place and verified (templates tracked, real files ignored).
- **NEXT:** Phase 2 — Kickoff (interview → write real config files from templates).
- **Decision needed before Phase 3/5:** how to write the grocery list to Apple Notes
  (the MCP has no Notes tool — see Risks), and the runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).
- New Phase 1 files are untracked (not committed yet) — commit when ready.

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[ ]`  ← NEXT
- [ ] Decide skill vs slash-command for the interactive interview
- [ ] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [ ] Include calendar-allowlist selection (show user's calendars, let them pick watched ones)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[ ]`
- [ ] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [ ] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [ ] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment
- [ ] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days
- [ ] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples
- [!] Write grocery list → **Apple Notes** (NO MCP tool — needs osascript or alt; see Risks)
- [ ] Schedule prep reminders → "Family Meals" list (create list if missing)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Writes go only to the "Family Meals" Reminders list + Apple Notes.

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[!] Apple Notes output:** apple-events MCP exposes only Calendar + Reminders — no Notes tool. Options: `osascript` (AppleScript) via Bash, the automating-mac-apps plugin, or a different MCP. Decide before Phase 3.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Progress Notes — Family Cookbook Workflow

A running diary of steps taken while building out this workflow.

## 2026-07-09
- Added `/Users/****/Documents/mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` as a working directory (project-scoped, saved to `07_2026_family-cookbook/.claude/settings.local.json`).
- Created blank `requirements.md` in the mp-docs folder.
- Created this progress notes file to passively record build steps.
- Researched Claude Code options for Apple Reminders integration. Two mechanisms: MCP servers vs. skills; two bridges: AppleScript (slow) vs. EventKit (fast). Top candidates: `rem` (EventKit skill, fastest, Reminders-only), FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar), automating-mac-apps plugin (broad Apple-app automation). All run locally on macOS and need Reminders permission.
- Inspected the automating-mac-apps plugin: 16 skills (Reminders + Calendar/Mail/Notes/office suite/etc.), AppleScript-based, community repo. Reminders skill is self-contained. Concluded it's overkill for a Reminders-focused workflow.
- **Decision:** build on FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar). Verified: Node v25.3.0 (needs 20+), package v1.5.0 ships pre-built signed binary (no Xcode via npx). Tools: reminders_tasks, reminders_subtasks, reminders_lists, calendar_events, calendar_calendars.
- Installed it project-scoped via `07_2026_family-cookbook/.mcp.json` (server name `apple-events`). Pending: restart/approve the MCP server in Claude Code, then grant macOS Reminders/Calendar permission (prompts as "event") on first call.
- Interviewed on requirements (2 rounds) and wrote `requirements.md` from the Project Plan. Key decisions: hybrid recipe sourcing (personal MD collection via link/photo extraction + AI/web gap-fill); grocery list grouped by ranked stores (full price optimization deferred to out-of-scope); configurable dinner count with the weekly run starting by reading Apple Calendar for dinners-out; config stored as project markdown files + shareable `.example` templates (real files gitignored), grocery list output to Apple Notes; **Apple Calendar** via apple-events instead of Google Calendar; end-of-week ratings prompt as part of a scheduled routine; recipes handed to mobile via Apple Notes; per-recipe prep reminders in a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list; portion/scaling strategy captured as a per-family kickoff config; weekly run = scheduled routine + manual trigger.
- Moved deferred items (full price optimization, breakfast/lunch, auto-ordering, nutrition tracking) into the Project Plan's out-of-scope section.
- Open follow-ups logged in requirements.md: Google→Apple Calendar migration, ratings file schema, scheduled-routine mechanics.
- Finalized the ratings loop: 1–5 scale **+ free-text notes** (notes carry the signal), one rating per recipe stored in that recipe's own frontmatter (dropped the standalone ratings file), used to boost/suppress and to surface *similar* highly-rated recipes from note patterns.
- Split scheduled automation into two independent jobs (new §5.8): recipe-reviewer (twice weekly, Wed+Fri PM) and meal-planner (kickoff-configured day/time, pre-drafts then waits for approval); both notify via push notification. Kickoff §5.1 gained a meal-planner run-timing config item.
- Vault `mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` became inaccessible (EPERM on read/ls even with the Claude Code sandbox disabled → macOS TCC / File-Provider access loss, not the sandbox). Fix = grant Full Disk Access to the terminal/Claude Code app.
- Worked around the lock by copying the docs (Project Plan, requirements, progress-notes) into the project at `07_2026_family-cookbook/docs/`. Promoted the updated requirements to `requirements.md`; archived the prior version as `requirements.old.md`. Project docs now live in-repo rather than the vault.

Learned: reminder titles reject emoji/em-dashes (plain text only); the MCP has **no Apple Notes tool** (Calendar + Reminders only), so grocery-list-to-Notes needs a separate mechanism (osascript / plugin / alt MCP) — flagged as a risk. Also confirmed calendar reads pull all calendars (sports/holiday noise) → will use a per-family "calendars to watch" allowlist; Calendar stays read-only, writes go only to a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list.
- Created `docs/build-tasks.md` as a phased build tracker (gitignored working doc) so work can pause/resume cleanly.

Rationale: matches the locked "one rating per recipe, overwritten" spec (§5.7), keeps recipe files simple, and the current note is what drives boost/suppress + find-similar.

content.text:
`/kickoff` is now registered as a command (I can see it in your skills list). Now the Apple Notes decision.

**The problem:** the `apple-events` MCP only does Calendar + Reminders — no Notes. So the grocery-list-to-Notes output (requirements §5.5) needs a different mechanism. I dug into the realistic options and they trade off along one axis: **zero-dependency vs. cleaner-interface vs. reuse-what-works.**

My honest lean is **osascript (AppleScript) via Bash** — Apple Notes has a full AppleScript dictionary, every Mac already has `osascript`, and it keeps the "clone and run" story dependency-free (which matters since you're sharing this with other families). The only cost is a one-time macOS *Automation* permission prompt, same flavor as the Calendar/Reminders one we already cleared. And I can verify it works immediately with a throwaway note, exactly like the MCP test.

But there are two other legitimate paths, so here's the call:

questions.question: How should the workflow write the weekly grocery list to Apple Notes (the MCP can't)?

options.label: osascript / AppleScript
options.description: Drive Apple Notes directly via `osascript` in Bash. No new dependencies (built into macOS), precise control (target folder, create/update the weekly note). One-time macOS Automation permission prompt. I can verify it now with a throwaway note.
options.preview:
osascript -e 'tell app "Notes" ...'
  → create/update a "Grocery List" note
  → in a "Family Meals" folder

Deps:   none (native to macOS)
Permit: 1 Automation prompt (once)
Share:  works on any Mac, no install
Risk:   AppleScript quoting/HTML is fiddly

options.label: Add a Notes MCP/plugin
options.description: Install a Notes-capable MCP server or the automating-mac-apps plugin to get structured notes_create/update tools. Cleaner interface, but a new dependency each shared family must install and trust.

Install Notes-capable MCP or
automating-mac-apps plugin
  → structured notes tools

Deps:   new dependency + trust

Upside: cleaner tool interface

options.description: Skip Notes: put the grocery list in a dedicated Apple Reminders 'Groceries' list (checkable while shopping) using the already-verified MCP. Diverges from the spec and loses the note-to-mobile handoff, but zero new work/permissions.

  no note-to-Claude-mobile handoff

content.text:
That's a sharp distinction — and you're right. A **checkable Reminders list is genuinely the better shopping UX** (tick items off in the aisle), while **Apple Notes is the right home for the recipes** themselves (they sync to your phone and open in Claude's mobile cooking UX). So the decision splits cleanly:

- **Grocery list → Apple Reminders** ("Groceries" list, checkable) — uses the MCP we already verified, zero new deps ✅
- **Recipes/menu → Apple Notes** — still needs a Notes-writing mechanism

That second half means the Notes question doesn't vanish — it just narrows to *recipes only*. For that, my recommendation stays **osascript/AppleScript** (no dependencies, native, shareable), and I can verify it with a throwaway note when you're ready.

Let me capture this split in the spec and tracker. First, re-reading the affected sections to edit precisely:

content.content:
116	
117	### 5.4 Grocery List (MVP)
118	
119	- Aggregate all needed ingredients across the week's recipes.
120	- Exclude items already on hand (pantry staples from kickoff).
121	- **Group the list by store** using simple ranked-store logic — e.g. if multiple
122	  recipes need chicken, assign chicken to the preferred bulk store (Costco).
123	- No live pricing/sale lookups in MVP (see Out of Scope).
124	
125	### 5.5 Outputs
126	
127	1. **Menu for the week, by day.**
128	2. **Recipes + step-by-step instructions.**
129	3. **Grocery list grouped by store**, delivered to **Apple Notes** (shared, so
130	   both partners can access; can be uploaded back into Claude for the guided
131	   cooking UX with timers).
132	4. **Prep reminders** in Apple Reminders (5.6).
133	
134	### 5.6 Prep Reminders
135	
136	- Written to a dedicated Apple Reminders list named **"Family Meals"**.
137	- **Per-recipe timing** based on what each recipe needs (e.g. defrost = night
138	  before, marinate = morning of).
139	
140	### 5.7 Ratings Feedback Loop
141	
142	- **Scale:** 1–5, with optional free-text notes (e.g. "loved the bowl format,"
143	  "too spicy," "halve the garlic next time"). The notes carry the real signal.
144	- **One rating per recipe** — the current rating, overwritten when re-rated —
145	  stored in the recipe's own markdown file (frontmatter), so the rating travels
146	  with the recipe and stays personal-but-shareable.
147	- **Captured by the recipe-reviewer job** (5.8), which prompts the family to
148	  rate recently cooked meals.
149	- **Usage in planning:**
150	  - Boost highly-rated recipes for re-suggestion.
151	  - Suppress low-rated recipes.
152	  - Use ratings **and notes** to find *similar* highly-rated recipes — the notes
153	    reveal the pattern the family likes. (Example: a note that they loved the
154	    "in a bowl" format of a taco bowl signals suggesting more "X in a bowl"
155	    meals, not just that specific taco recipe.)
156	- **Frontmatter schema** (lives in each recipe file, alongside `title`, `source`,
157	  `servings`, `prep_time`):
158	
159	  ```yaml
160	  rating: 4                             # 1–5, current only (overwritten on re-rate)
161	  rating_notes: "loved the bowl format" # free text; carries the real signal
162	  liked_traits: [bowl-format, one-pan]  # structured hooks for "find similar"
163	  last_cooked: 2026-07-08
164	  times_cooked: 3                       # increments each time the recipe is planned
165	  ```
166	
167	### 5.8 Scheduled Automation
168	
169	Two **independent** scheduled jobs, kept separate so feedback is collected more
170	frequently and feeds the next week's planning. Both notify via **push
171	notification**.
172	
173	1. **Recipe reviewer** — runs **twice weekly, Wednesday and Friday evenings**.
174	   Prompts the family to rate recently cooked meals (5.7). The higher review
175	   cadence keeps ratings fresh for the next planning run.
176	2. **Meal planner** — runs at the family's **preferred day/time (set at kickoff;
177	   5.1)**. **Pre-drafts** the week's plan from the calendar and preferences, then
178	   waits for the family's review/approval before finalizing. Also runnable
179	   manually (5.2).
180	
181	**Failure & no-response handling:**
182	
183	- **Empty calendar** (meal planner can't tell who's home): draft the plan anyway
184	  using the configured default dinner count and assuming the **full family is
185	  home every night**, and flag the assumption prominently at the top of the
186	  output ("Calendar was empty — assumed full family home all week; edit &
187	  re-run if wrong").
188	- **No approval by deadline** (meal planner): if the pre-drafted plan isn't

file.content:
### 5.4 Grocery List (MVP)

- Aggregate all needed ingredients across the week's recipes.
- Exclude items already on hand (pantry staples from kickoff).
- **Group the list by store** using simple ranked-store logic — e.g. if multiple
  recipes need chicken, assign chicken to the preferred bulk store (Costco).
- No live pricing/sale lookups in MVP (see Out of Scope).

### 5.5 Outputs

1. **Menu for the week, by day.**
2. **Recipes + step-by-step instructions.**
3. **Grocery list grouped by store**, delivered to **Apple Notes** (shared, so
   both partners can access; can be uploaded back into Claude for the guided
   cooking UX with timers).
4. **Prep reminders** in Apple Reminders (5.6).

### 5.6 Prep Reminders

- Written to a dedicated Apple Reminders list named **"Family Meals"**.
- **Per-recipe timing** based on what each recipe needs (e.g. defrost = night
  before, marinate = morning of).

### 5.7 Ratings Feedback Loop

- **Scale:** 1–5, with optional free-text notes (e.g. "loved the bowl format,"
  "too spicy," "halve the garlic next time"). The notes carry the real signal.
- **One rating per recipe** — the current rating, overwritten when re-rated —
  stored in the recipe's own markdown file (frontmatter), so the rating travels
  with the recipe and stays personal-but-shareable.
- **Captured by the recipe-reviewer job** (5.8), which prompts the family to
  rate recently cooked meals.
- **Usage in planning:**
  - Boost highly-rated recipes for re-suggestion.
  - Suppress low-rated recipes.
  - Use ratings **and notes** to find *similar* highly-rated recipes — the notes
    reveal the pattern the family likes. (Example: a note that they loved the
    "in a bowl" format of a taco bowl signals suggesting more "X in a bowl"
    meals, not just that specific taco recipe.)
- **Frontmatter schema** (lives in each recipe file, alongside `title`, `source`,
  `servings`, `prep_time`):

  ```yaml
  rating: 4                             # 1–5, current only (overwritten on re-rate)
  rating_notes: "loved the bowl format" # free text; carries the real signal
  liked_traits: [bowl-format, one-pan]  # structured hooks for "find similar"
  last_cooked: 2026-07-08
  times_cooked: 3                       # increments each time the recipe is planned
  ```

### 5.8 Scheduled Automation

Two **independent** scheduled jobs, kept separate so feedback is collected more
frequently and feeds the next week's planning. Both notify via **push
notification**.

1. **Recipe reviewer** — runs **twice weekly, Wednesday and Friday evenings**.
   Prompts the family to rate recently cooked meals (5.7). The higher review
   cadence keeps ratings fresh for the next planning run.
2. **Meal planner** — runs at the family's **preferred day/time (set at kickoff;
   5.1)**. **Pre-drafts** the week's plan from the calendar and preferences, then
   waits for the family's review/approval before finalizing. Also runnable
   manually (5.2).

**Failure & no-response handling:**

- **Empty calendar** (meal planner can't tell who's home): draft the plan anyway
  using the configured default dinner count and assuming the **full family is
  home every night**, and flag the assumption prominently at the top of the
  output ("Calendar was empty — assumed full family home all week; edit &
  re-run if wrong").
- **No approval by deadline** (meal planner): if the pre-drafted plan isn't
  approved by the **evening before the family's shopping day** (5.1),
  **auto-finalize the draft as-is** — write the grocery list to Apple Notes and

- **No approval by deadline** (meal planner): if the pre-drafted plan isn't
  approved by the **evening before the family's shopping day** (5.1),
  **auto-finalize the draft as-is** — write the grocery list to Apple Notes and
  schedule prep reminders — so shopping isn't blocked.
- **No response to a ratings prompt** (recipe reviewer): no rating is captured
  that cycle (harmless); the next scheduled run re-prompts.

---

## 6. Integrations & Platform

- **Claude Desktop** — build and run platform; user-facing interface. Claude's
  mobile app provides the guided recipe/cooking UX (timers) when a recipe note
  is uploaded.
- **apple-events MCP** (`FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events`, installed
  project-scoped via `.mcp.json`) — reads **Apple Calendar** (schedule) and
  writes **Apple Reminders** (the "Family Meals" prep list).
  - Note: this changes the original plan's "Google Calendar" — schedule lives in
    Apple Calendar (confirmed).
- **Apple Notes** — destination for the weekly grocery list and recipe handoff to
  mobile.
- **GitHub** — the workflow is backed up as a shareable project so other
  households can clone and run it.

---

## 7. Data & Storage Model

All persistent config and state live as **markdown files in the project** so they
can be edited directly or by chatting with Claude, and version-controlled.

- **Template files** (committed, shareable): `*.example.md` with placeholder
  values and field explanations — e.g. `config/household.example.md`,
  `config/pantry-staples.example.md`, `config/store-rankings.example.md`.
- **Real files** (gitignored — personal data): the filled-in versions Claude
  reads/writes — e.g. `config/household.md`, etc.
- **Recipe collection:** structured markdown files, one per recipe. Each recipe's
  rating (1–5) and notes live in that file's frontmatter (5.7).
- **Grocery list output:** Apple Notes (not a project file).

content.text:
That's the **official Anthropic "Read and Write Apple Notes" extension** (DXT v0.1.7) — and it exposes exactly what we need:

- `add_note` — create a note
- `update_note_content` — update an existing note
- `list_notes` / `get_note_content` — read

So yes, you already have a clean, official Notes writer installed. This is **better than raw osascript** for the recipes→Notes job (structured tools, Anthropic-maintained, MIT). One important nuance, though:

**It's a Claude *Desktop* extension — not currently wired into this Claude Code session.** (My live tools here are only the `apple-events` MCP; no Notes tools loaded.) So there are two ways to use it:
- **Run the meal-planner in Claude Desktop** → the extension auto-loads there and "just works."
- **Wire it into the project `.mcp.json`** (like we did for `apple-events`) so Claude Code *and scheduled runs* can call it — but its path lives under `~/Library/.../<your-username>/...`, which is **machine-specific** and hurts the "clone-and-share" story.

Let me record the finding in the spec/tracker, then put the decision to you:

  `osascript`/AppleScript (mechanism to be verified; needs a one-time macOS Automation
  grant).
input.new_string:
- **Apple Notes** — destination for the week's **recipes/menu**, handed to the phone for
  the mobile guided-cooking UX. The apple-events MCP has no Notes tool, so this uses the
  official **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** Claude extension (Anthropic DXT; tools
  `add_note` / `update_note_content`; already installed in Claude Desktop) — with
  `osascript`/AppleScript as a no-install fallback for portability. Wiring for
  Claude-Code/scheduled runs is TBD (its path is machine-specific); needs a one-time macOS
  Automation grant either way.

# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-09

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 done and **committed** (`963a8b1`). Phase 2 mechanism built: the
  `/kickoff` slash-command is ready to run (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`, untracked).
- **NEXT (your call):** run `/kickoff` to fill in your real config, OR start Phase 3
  (weekly meal-planner) — but Phase 3 first needs the Apple Notes decision below.
- **Decisions still parked:** Apple Notes output mechanism (Phase 3), runtime/scheduling
  home (Phase 5). See Risks.

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[~]`  (mechanism built; run pending)
- [x] Decide skill vs slash-command → slash-command `/kickoff` (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`)
- [x] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [x] Include calendar-allowlist selection (lists user's calendars via MCP, user picks watched)
- [ ] **Run `/kickoff` for real** to write `config/*.md` (user-driven, interactive; when ready)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[ ]`
- [ ] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [ ] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [ ] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment
- [ ] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days
- [ ] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples
- [!] Write grocery list → **Apple Notes** (NO MCP tool — needs osascript or alt; see Risks)
- [ ] Schedule prep reminders → "Family Meals" list (create list if missing)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Writes go only to the "Family Meals" Reminders list + Apple Notes.

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[!] Apple Notes output:** apple-events MCP exposes only Calendar + Reminders — no Notes tool. Options: `osascript` (AppleScript) via Bash, the automating-mac-apps plugin, or a different MCP. Decide before Phase 3.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

+- **[~] Recipes → Apple Notes:** grocery list moved to Reminders, so Notes is now needed only for recipes/menu. User already has the official **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** Claude extension installed (Anthropic DXT: `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — but it's a Claude *Desktop* extension, not wired into Claude Code. Options: (a) run planner in Claude Desktop where it auto-loads, (b) add it to `.mcp.json` for Claude Code (machine-specific path — bad for sharing), (c) `osascript` fallback (no install, portable). Decide before Phase 3; verify with a throwaway note.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-09

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 done and **committed** (`963a8b1`). Phase 2 mechanism built: the
  `/kickoff` slash-command is ready to run (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`, untracked).
- **NEXT (your call):** run `/kickoff` to fill in your real config, OR start Phase 3
  (weekly meal-planner) — but Phase 3 first needs the Apple Notes decision below.
- **Outputs decided:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (via MCP); recipes/menu →
  Apple Notes (mechanism narrowing — Notes extension vs osascript; see Risks).
  **Still parked:** runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[~]`  (mechanism built; run pending)
- [x] Decide skill vs slash-command → slash-command `/kickoff` (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`)
- [x] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [x] Include calendar-allowlist selection (lists user's calendars via MCP, user picks watched)
- [ ] **Run `/kickoff` for real** to write `config/*.md` (user-driven, interactive; when ready)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[ ]`
- [ ] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [ ] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [ ] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment
- [ ] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days
- [ ] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples
- [ ] Write grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries" list** (checkable; via MCP)
- [ ] Schedule prep reminders → Reminders **"Family Meals"** list (create if missing)
- [ ] Write recipes/menu → **Apple Notes** (Notes extension or osascript — see Risks)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Writes go only to the "Family Meals" Reminders list + Apple Notes.

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[~] Recipes → Apple Notes:** grocery list moved to Reminders, so Notes is now needed only for recipes/menu. User already has the official **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** Claude extension installed (Anthropic DXT: `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — but it's a Claude *Desktop* extension, not wired into Claude Code. Options: (a) run planner in Claude Desktop where it auto-loads, (b) add it to `.mcp.json` for Claude Code (machine-specific path — bad for sharing), (c) `osascript` fallback (no install, portable). Decide before Phase 3; verify with a throwaway note.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

# Progress Notes — Family Cookbook Workflow

A running diary of steps taken while building out this workflow.

## 2026-07-09
- Added `/Users/****/Documents/mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` as a working directory (project-scoped, saved to `07_2026_family-cookbook/.claude/settings.local.json`).
- Created blank `requirements.md` in the mp-docs folder.
- Created this progress notes file to passively record build steps.
- Researched Claude Code options for Apple Reminders integration. Two mechanisms: MCP servers vs. skills; two bridges: AppleScript (slow) vs. EventKit (fast). Top candidates: `rem` (EventKit skill, fastest, Reminders-only), FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar), automating-mac-apps plugin (broad Apple-app automation). All run locally on macOS and need Reminders permission.
- Inspected the automating-mac-apps plugin: 16 skills (Reminders + Calendar/Mail/Notes/office suite/etc.), AppleScript-based, community repo. Reminders skill is self-contained. Concluded it's overkill for a Reminders-focused workflow.
- **Decision:** build on FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar). Verified: Node v25.3.0 (needs 20+), package v1.5.0 ships pre-built signed binary (no Xcode via npx). Tools: reminders_tasks, reminders_subtasks, reminders_lists, calendar_events, calendar_calendars.
- Installed it project-scoped via `07_2026_family-cookbook/.mcp.json` (server name `apple-events`). Pending: restart/approve the MCP server in Claude Code, then grant macOS Reminders/Calendar permission (prompts as "event") on first call.
- Interviewed on requirements (2 rounds) and wrote `requirements.md` from the Project Plan. Key decisions: hybrid recipe sourcing (personal MD collection via link/photo extraction + AI/web gap-fill); grocery list grouped by ranked stores (full price optimization deferred to out-of-scope); configurable dinner count with the weekly run starting by reading Apple Calendar for dinners-out; config stored as project markdown files + shareable `.example` templates (real files gitignored), grocery list output to Apple Notes; **Apple Calendar** via apple-events instead of Google Calendar; end-of-week ratings prompt as part of a scheduled routine; recipes handed to mobile via Apple Notes; per-recipe prep reminders in a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list; portion/scaling strategy captured as a per-family kickoff config; weekly run = scheduled routine + manual trigger.
- Moved deferred items (full price optimization, breakfast/lunch, auto-ordering, nutrition tracking) into the Project Plan's out-of-scope section.
- Open follow-ups logged in requirements.md: Google→Apple Calendar migration, ratings file schema, scheduled-routine mechanics.
- Finalized the ratings loop: 1–5 scale **+ free-text notes** (notes carry the signal), one rating per recipe stored in that recipe's own frontmatter (dropped the standalone ratings file), used to boost/suppress and to surface *similar* highly-rated recipes from note patterns.
- Split scheduled automation into two independent jobs (new §5.8): recipe-reviewer (twice weekly, Wed+Fri PM) and meal-planner (kickoff-configured day/time, pre-drafts then waits for approval); both notify via push notification. Kickoff §5.1 gained a meal-planner run-timing config item.
- Vault `mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` became inaccessible (EPERM on read/ls even with the Claude Code sandbox disabled → macOS TCC / File-Provider access loss, not the sandbox). Fix = grant Full Disk Access to the terminal/Claude Code app.
- Worked around the lock by copying the docs (Project Plan, requirements, progress-notes) into the project at `07_2026_family-cookbook/docs/`. Promoted the updated requirements to `requirements.md`; archived the prior version as `requirements.old.md`. Project docs now live in-repo rather than the vault.
- Git hygiene: staged `docs/requirements.md` (the shareable spec) + `.gitignore`; gitignored the personal/working docs (`Project Plan.md`, `progress-notes.md`, `requirements.old.md`).
- **Verified the apple-events MCP end-to-end:** Calendar read (23 calendars, weekly events) and Reminders create+delete all work; no permission errors (macOS access already granted). Learned: reminder titles reject emoji/em-dashes (plain text only); the MCP has **no Apple Notes tool** (Calendar + Reminders only), so grocery-list-to-Notes needs a separate mechanism (osascript / plugin / alt MCP) — flagged as a risk. Also confirmed calendar reads pull all calendars (sports/holiday noise) → will use a per-family "calendars to watch" allowlist; Calendar stays read-only, writes go only to a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list.
- Created `docs/build-tasks.md` as a phased build tracker (gitignored working doc) so work can pause/resume cleanly.
- **Phase 1 (Foundation) built.** Split config templates: `config/household.example.md` (address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy, default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist), plus `dietary`, `pantry-staples`, `store-rankings`, `equipment`. Recipe schema `recipes/_template.md` uses structured frontmatter ingredients (qty/unit/aisle/staple) to drive grocery aggregation, plus the locked ratings fields. Two seed recipes (sheet-pan chicken fajitas, beef taco bowls) that deliberately share peppers/onion/lime/spices to exercise shared-ingredient logic. `.gitignore` now ignores real `config/*.md` while keeping `*.example.md` templates + the recipe collection tracked — verified with `git check-ignore`.
- **Decision (ratings notes):** keep a single *current* note per recipe (`rating_notes`, overwritten on re-rate) — considered but declined an append-only `rating_history` log. Rationale: matches the locked "one rating per recipe, overwritten" spec (§5.7), keeps recipe files simple, and the current note is what drives boost/suppress + find-similar.
- **Committed Phase 1** to `main` (`963a8b1`, 10 files): config templates, recipe schema + 2 seeds, `.gitignore`, and `requirements.md`. Gitignored working docs correctly excluded.
- **Built `/kickoff`** as a project slash-command (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`): reads the `.example` templates, interviews in small groups (basics → members+dietary → kitchen → shopping → scheduling → optional favorite recipes), lists the user's real calendars via the apple-events MCP so they choose the watch allowlist, then writes the gitignored `config/*.md` files after confirmation. Mechanism only — actually running it (collecting real personal data) is a separate user-driven step. Currently untracked/uncommitted.
- Resolved the last two §9 open items. **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts anyway with defaults (configured dinner count + full family home) and flags the assumption. **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize the draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created); added a **shopping day** field to kickoff §5.1 to anchor that deadline. **Ratings frontmatter schema** locked: `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (structured find-similar tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`. §9 now has no open items — requirements are build-ready.

content.text:
Docs are updated to reflect the split (grocery→Reminders, recipes→Notes) and the extension finding. Now my recommendation — and there's one factor that tips it:

## The deciding factor: scheduled runs
The recipes→Notes write doesn't only happen when you're sitting in front of Claude — it also fires inside the **scheduled** meal-planner job (Phase 5). And there's the rub:

- A Claude **Desktop extension isn't available to a headless/scheduled job**, and wiring it into `.mcp.json` bakes in your `~/Library/.../<username>/` path — which breaks the clone-and-share goal.
- **`osascript` runs everywhere** — interactive Claude Code, scheduled runs, *and* any Mac with zero install.

Critically, **`osascript` is runtime-agnostic**: it works whether you later decide to schedule via Claude Code *or* run interactively in Desktop. Committing to the extension now would quietly pre-commit you to the Desktop-interactive path.

## Recommendation
- **Primary: `osascript`** for the recipes→Notes writer — I encapsulate the AppleScript once inside the planner, so you're not hand-writing it day to day. Portable, survives scheduling, no per-user path.
- **Keep the Notes extension** as a nice convenience for ad-hoc "put this note in Notes" moments in Desktop — just not the pipeline's dependency.

You get the outcome you wanted (not hand-rolling AppleScript yourself) without chaining the automation to a Desktop-only, machine-specific extension.

---

**Shall I verify `osascript`→Notes right now** with a throwaway note? ⚠️ First run triggers a one-time macOS prompt — *"…wants to control Notes"* — click **Allow**. That closes the mechanism with real evidence, exactly like the MCP test.

Or, if you'd rather commit to running the planner in **Claude Desktop** and lean on the installed extension, say so and I'll wire that path instead.

Well, just to make sure im following: 
"- A Claude Desktop extension isn't available to a headless/scheduled job, and wiring it into .mcp.json bakes in your ~/Library/.../<username>/ path — which breaks the clone-and-share goal.
- osascript runs everywhere — interactive Claude Code, scheduled runs, and any Mac with zero install." 

would this matter for this workflow? I expected to run the routine as a scheduled run locally, since my receipies and reminders will be accessed localy. Or tell me if that wasn't going to work for another reason that im not thinking of

content.text:
Great question to pause on — let me be precise and also correct a spot where I overstated.

## Your instinct is right — in fact it's *required*, not just fine
This workflow reads Calendar/Reminders and writes Reminders/Notes through **local macOS** (EventKit + Apple automation). A **cloud-scheduled** agent runs on a remote machine and literally can't see your Mac's Calendar or Notes. So "run it locally" isn't just your preference — it's the only thing that works here. Good instinct.

## What "scheduled locally" actually means mechanically
That's the Phase 5 runtime question, and the honest answer is: a **local scheduler on your Mac** (macOS `launchd`/`cron`) that wakes up and runs **Claude Code headlessly**. (Claude *Desktop* has no built-in scheduler, and cloud routines can't reach local apps — so this is the path.)

## Does the osascript-vs-extension choice actually matter for you? Honestly — barely.
For your **personal** use, both run on your Mac and both work. Let me walk back my "breaks clone-and-share" line — that was too strong:
- In a **headless scheduled run**, the Notes DXT isn't auto-loaded (it's a Desktop thing), but you *can* wire it into `.mcp.json` and point at it with `$HOME/Library/...` so it's **not** hardcoded to your username. So it's shareable-ish — as long as each family also installs that DXT.
- `osascript` just needs nothing — no wiring, no install, works in the headless run and on any Mac.

So the real difference is small: **osascript = zero setup + most portable; the extension = cleaner tool calls but one `.mcp.json` entry + a per-family install.** For you alone, pick whichever you like.

## The gotcha you *might* not be thinking of (and it's the important one)
Both Notes mechanisms — osascript *and* the DXT — drive Apple Notes through **macOS UI automation**. That means for a scheduled run, your **Mac has to be awake and logged in** at that moment, or the Notes write can fail. Calendar/Reminders (via EventKit in the MCP) are far more robust headlessly. So the **recipes→Notes step is the most fragile part of the automation regardless of which mechanism you pick** — worth designing for (e.g., run the schedule at a time the Mac is on, and have the planner note if the Notes write didn't land).

## Bottom line
- Local scheduled run: ✅ correct and necessary.
- Notes mechanism: your call — I lean `osascript` purely for zero-friction, but if you'd enjoy the extension's clean tools for your own machine, that's totally fine and I'll wire it.
- Either way, we should make the Notes write **non-blocking** so a locked-Mac hiccup never breaks the grocery/reminders part.

Want me to **verify `osascript`→Notes now** (throwaway note; one-time "control Notes" prompt), or would you rather I wire up the **extension** path? And separately — good to know we've effectively answered Phase 5's runtime question: **local `launchd`/`cron` → headless Claude Code.**

message.content: what are some alternatives to using notes for this step in the workflow? The recipe part? Should we use google docs instead, is there a more reliable way to update content in a google doc?

content.text:
Good — this is worth stepping back on, because you've identified that the *delivery channel* is the fragile link, and it's worth picking the right one rather than forcing Notes.

## First, the key design move (independent of channel)
Separate **generate** from **deliver**. The planner should **always write each recipe/menu as a markdown file in the repo** — that's the source of truth, version-controlled, and it *never* fails. "Push to Notes/Docs/wherever" becomes a thin, best-effort layer on top. That way a locked-Mac hiccup on the delivery step loses nothing — you just re-run delivery or open the file. This makes whatever channel you pick non-critical.

## Alternatives for the mobile recipe view

| Channel | Reliability for automated updates | Mobile / cooking UX | Sharing to other families |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Apple Notes** (current) | ⚠️ Uses macOS UI automation — Mac must be awake/logged in | ✅ Native, great; hand to Claude mobile | ✅ Every Apple family already has it |
| **Google Docs** | ✅ Proper REST API — headless, even cloud, no Mac-awake needed | ✅ Docs app; shareable link | ⚠️ Needs Google account + OAuth setup |
| **Email (Gmail)** | ✅ Very reliable API | ⚠️ Fine to read, clutters inbox, weak cooking UX | ✅ Everyone has email |
| **Repo markdown + Obsidian sync** | ✅ File-based, fully reliable | ⚠️ You already use Obsidian; view on phone, but handing to Claude is manual | ✅ No new dependency |

## Directly answering your Google Docs question
**Yes — Google Docs is meaningfully more reliable than Notes for automated updates**, because it's a real API (no local UI automation, no "Mac must be awake"). It could even run from a cloud schedule.

**And yes, there's a much more reliable way to update a Doc than the naive approach.** The Docs API's *fragile* part is index-based editing (you compute character positions to delete/insert — easy to get wrong). Two robust patterns avoid that entirely:
1. **New doc per week** — `create` + insert once into a "Weekly Dinners" folder. Trivially reliable, and you get a clean archive of past weeks. The planner just hands you the new link.
2. **Template + `replaceAllText`** — keep a doc/template with `{{MENU}}`, `{{RECIPES}}` tokens; each week copy it and call `replaceAllText` (token → content). No index math, keeps a stable link.

Both are far more reliable than delete-range/insert.

## Honest tradeoffs / caveats
- **Google = a second ecosystem.** Everything else here is Apple (Calendar, Reminders, iPhone). Adding Google Docs means an OAuth setup for you, and more friction for families you share with. I'd also need to confirm the Google Docs *write* tooling is actually available/authorized (the listed Google MCP tools were auth-only so far).
- The **"open in Claude mobile for timers"** UX works from any pasted text, so it isn't locked to Apple Notes — a Doc works too.

## My take
- **Want max reliability + don't mind Google:** Google Docs, **new-doc-per-week** (simplest, most robust). This genuinely fixes the fragility.
- **Want to stay all-Apple + easiest sharing:** keep **Apple Notes**, but make it non-blocking and accept the Mac-awake caveat.
- **Zero new dependency:** lean on the **repo markdown** you're already generating (+ your existing Obsidian for a phone view).

Which ecosystem do you want the recipe delivery to live in — **Apple**, **Google**, or **just the repo/Obsidian you already have**? Once you pick, I'll wire it (and for Google, set up + verify the Docs auth path first). And remember the source-of-truth markdown means we can even support more than one channel cheaply.

# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-09

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 done and **committed** (`963a8b1`). Phase 2 mechanism built: the
  `/kickoff` slash-command is ready to run (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`, untracked).
- **NEXT (your call):** run `/kickoff` to fill in your real config, OR start Phase 3
  (weekly meal-planner) — but Phase 3 first needs the Apple Notes decision below.
- **Outputs decided:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (via MCP); recipes/menu →
  Apple Notes (mechanism narrowing — Notes extension vs osascript; see Risks).
  **Still parked:** runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[~]`  (mechanism built; run pending)
- [x] Decide skill vs slash-command → slash-command `/kickoff` (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`)
- [x] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [x] Include calendar-allowlist selection (lists user's calendars via MCP, user picks watched)
- [ ] **Run `/kickoff` for real** to write `config/*.md` (user-driven, interactive; when ready)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[ ]`
- [ ] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [ ] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [ ] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment
- [ ] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days
- [ ] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples
- [ ] Write grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries" list** (checkable; via MCP)
- [ ] Schedule prep reminders → Reminders **"Family Meals"** list (create if missing)
- [ ] Write recipes/menu → **Apple Notes** (Notes extension or osascript — see Risks)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Writes go only to Apple Reminders ("Family Meals" prep + "Groceries" shopping) and Apple Notes (recipes/menu).
- **Output split:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (checkable = better shopping UX); recipes/menu → Apple Notes (mobile cooking UX); prep reminders → Reminders "Family Meals".

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[~] Recipes → Apple Notes:** grocery list moved to Reminders, so Notes is now needed only for recipes/menu. User already has the official **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** Claude extension installed (Anthropic DXT: `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — but it's a Claude *Desktop* extension, not wired into Claude Code. Options: (a) run planner in Claude Desktop where it auto-loads, (b) add it to `.mcp.json` for Claude Code (machine-specific path — bad for sharing), (c) `osascript` fallback (no install, portable). Decide before Phase 3; verify with a throwaway note.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

-- [ ] Write recipes/menu → **Apple Notes** (Notes extension or osascript — see Risks)
+- [ ] Write recipes → **Google Docs** (one Doc per dinner in a shared weekly folder; create-only)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-09

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 done and **committed** (`963a8b1`). Phase 2 mechanism built: the
  `/kickoff` slash-command is ready to run (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`, untracked).
- **NEXT (your call):** run `/kickoff` to fill in your real config, verify **Google Docs
  auth** (gates Phase 3's recipe step), OR start the non-recipe parts of Phase 3.
- **Outputs decided:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (via MCP); recipes → **Google
  Docs**, one Doc per dinner in a shared weekly folder (create-only). Gating step: Google
  OAuth + confirm Docs write tooling. **Still parked:** runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[~]`  (mechanism built; run pending)
- [x] Decide skill vs slash-command → slash-command `/kickoff` (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`)
- [x] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [x] Include calendar-allowlist selection (lists user's calendars via MCP, user picks watched)
- [ ] **Run `/kickoff` for real** to write `config/*.md` (user-driven, interactive; when ready)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[ ]`
- [ ] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [ ] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [ ] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment
- [ ] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days
- [ ] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples
- [ ] Write grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries" list** (checkable; via MCP)
- [ ] Schedule prep reminders → Reminders **"Family Meals"** list (create if missing)
- [ ] Write recipes → **Google Docs** (one Doc per dinner in a shared weekly folder; create-only)
- [ ] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline
- [ ] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Writes go only to Apple Reminders ("Family Meals" prep + "Groceries" shopping) and Apple Notes (recipes/menu).
- **Output split:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (checkable = better shopping UX); recipes/menu → Apple Notes (mobile cooking UX); prep reminders → Reminders "Family Meals".

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[~] Recipes → Apple Notes:** grocery list moved to Reminders, so Notes is now needed only for recipes/menu. User already has the official **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** Claude extension installed (Anthropic DXT: `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — but it's a Claude *Desktop* extension, not wired into Claude Code. Options: (a) run planner in Claude Desktop where it auto-loads, (b) add it to `.mcp.json` for Claude Code (machine-specific path — bad for sharing), (c) `osascript` fallback (no install, portable). Decide before Phase 3; verify with a throwaway note.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

# Progress Notes — Family Cookbook Workflow

A running diary of steps taken while building out this workflow.

## 2026-07-09
- Added `/Users/****/Documents/mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` as a working directory (project-scoped, saved to `07_2026_family-cookbook/.claude/settings.local.json`).
- Created blank `requirements.md` in the mp-docs folder.
- Created this progress notes file to passively record build steps.
- Researched Claude Code options for Apple Reminders integration. Two mechanisms: MCP servers vs. skills; two bridges: AppleScript (slow) vs. EventKit (fast). Top candidates: `rem` (EventKit skill, fastest, Reminders-only), FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar), automating-mac-apps plugin (broad Apple-app automation). All run locally on macOS and need Reminders permission.
- Inspected the automating-mac-apps plugin: 16 skills (Reminders + Calendar/Mail/Notes/office suite/etc.), AppleScript-based, community repo. Reminders skill is self-contained. Concluded it's overkill for a Reminders-focused workflow.
- **Decision:** build on FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar). Verified: Node v25.3.0 (needs 20+), package v1.5.0 ships pre-built signed binary (no Xcode via npx). Tools: reminders_tasks, reminders_subtasks, reminders_lists, calendar_events, calendar_calendars.
- Installed it project-scoped via `07_2026_family-cookbook/.mcp.json` (server name `apple-events`). Pending: restart/approve the MCP server in Claude Code, then grant macOS Reminders/Calendar permission (prompts as "event") on first call.
- Interviewed on requirements (2 rounds) and wrote `requirements.md` from the Project Plan. Key decisions: hybrid recipe sourcing (personal MD collection via link/photo extraction + AI/web gap-fill); grocery list grouped by ranked stores (full price optimization deferred to out-of-scope); configurable dinner count with the weekly run starting by reading Apple Calendar for dinners-out; config stored as project markdown files + shareable `.example` templates (real files gitignored), grocery list output to Apple Notes; **Apple Calendar** via apple-events instead of Google Calendar; end-of-week ratings prompt as part of a scheduled routine; recipes handed to mobile via Apple Notes; per-recipe prep reminders in a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list; portion/scaling strategy captured as a per-family kickoff config; weekly run = scheduled routine + manual trigger.
- Moved deferred items (full price optimization, breakfast/lunch, auto-ordering, nutrition tracking) into the Project Plan's out-of-scope section.
- Open follow-ups logged in requirements.md: Google→Apple Calendar migration, ratings file schema, scheduled-routine mechanics.
- Finalized the ratings loop: 1–5 scale **+ free-text notes** (notes carry the signal), one rating per recipe stored in that recipe's own frontmatter (dropped the standalone ratings file), used to boost/suppress and to surface *similar* highly-rated recipes from note patterns.
- Split scheduled automation into two independent jobs (new §5.8): recipe-reviewer (twice weekly, Wed+Fri PM) and meal-planner (kickoff-configured day/time, pre-drafts then waits for approval); both notify via push notification. Kickoff §5.1 gained a meal-planner run-timing config item.
- Vault `mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` became inaccessible (EPERM on read/ls even with the Claude Code sandbox disabled → macOS TCC / File-Provider access loss, not the sandbox). Fix = grant Full Disk Access to the terminal/Claude Code app.
- Worked around the lock by copying the docs (Project Plan, requirements, progress-notes) into the project at `07_2026_family-cookbook/docs/`. Promoted the updated requirements to `requirements.md`; archived the prior version as `requirements.old.md`. Project docs now live in-repo rather than the vault.
- Git hygiene: staged `docs/requirements.md` (the shareable spec) + `.gitignore`; gitignored the personal/working docs (`Project Plan.md`, `progress-notes.md`, `requirements.old.md`).
- **Verified the apple-events MCP end-to-end:** Calendar read (23 calendars, weekly events) and Reminders create+delete all work; no permission errors (macOS access already granted). Learned: reminder titles reject emoji/em-dashes (plain text only); the MCP has **no Apple Notes tool** (Calendar + Reminders only), so grocery-list-to-Notes needs a separate mechanism (osascript / plugin / alt MCP) — flagged as a risk. Also confirmed calendar reads pull all calendars (sports/holiday noise) → will use a per-family "calendars to watch" allowlist; Calendar stays read-only, writes go only to a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list.
- Created `docs/build-tasks.md` as a phased build tracker (gitignored working doc) so work can pause/resume cleanly.
- **Phase 1 (Foundation) built.** Split config templates: `config/household.example.md` (address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy, default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist), plus `dietary`, `pantry-staples`, `store-rankings`, `equipment`. Recipe schema `recipes/_template.md` uses structured frontmatter ingredients (qty/unit/aisle/staple) to drive grocery aggregation, plus the locked ratings fields. Two seed recipes (sheet-pan chicken fajitas, beef taco bowls) that deliberately share peppers/onion/lime/spices to exercise shared-ingredient logic. `.gitignore` now ignores real `config/*.md` while keeping `*.example.md` templates + the recipe collection tracked — verified with `git check-ignore`.
- **Decision (ratings notes):** keep a single *current* note per recipe (`rating_notes`, overwritten on re-rate) — considered but declined an append-only `rating_history` log. Rationale: matches the locked "one rating per recipe, overwritten" spec (§5.7), keeps recipe files simple, and the current note is what drives boost/suppress + find-similar.
- **Committed Phase 1** to `main` (`963a8b1`, 10 files): config templates, recipe schema + 2 seeds, `.gitignore`, and `requirements.md`. Gitignored working docs correctly excluded.
- **Decision (outputs) + finding:** split the mobile destinations — grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries"** list (checkable; better shopping UX; uses the working MCP, no new deps); recipes/menu → Apple **Notes** (phone cooking UX). The apple-events MCP can't write Notes. Discovered the user already has the official Anthropic **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** DXT installed (Claude Desktop; `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — cleaner than raw osascript, but it's a Desktop extension not wired into Claude Code (machine-specific path, awkward for sharing) and won't be present in headless scheduled runs. Open: run planner in Claude Desktop (extension auto-loads) vs. `.mcp.json` vs. osascript (portable). Requirements §5.4/5.5/5.6/6/7 updated.
- **Built `/kickoff`** as a project slash-command (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`): reads the `.example` templates, interviews in small groups (basics → members+dietary → kitchen → shopping → scheduling → optional favorite recipes), lists the user's real calendars via the apple-events MCP so they choose the watch allowlist, then writes the gitignored `config/*.md` files after confirmation. Mechanism only — actually running it (collecting real personal data) is a separate user-driven step. Currently untracked/uncommitted.
- Resolved the last two §9 open items. **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts anyway with defaults (configured dinner count + full family home) and flags the assumption. **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize the draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created); added a **shopping day** field to kickoff §5.1 to anchor that deadline. **Ratings frontmatter schema** locked: `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (structured find-similar tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`. §9 now has no open items — requirements are build-ready.

Captured everywhere. Here's how I'll structure it, plus the one thing to verify before we can build it.

## Design defaults I'll use (say if you'd change any)
- **Grouping + one-time sharing:** daily docs live in a shared Drive folder — `Family Dinners / Week of 2026-07-13 /`. You share the top **`Family Dinners`** folder with your partner *once*, and they automatically see every week and day (beats sharing each doc).
- **Naming for scanning:** `Mon 07-13 — Beef Taco Bowls` → sorts by day, night is obvious at a glance.
- **Leftover nights** (your `deliberate-leftovers` strategy) won't spawn a duplicate — they point back to the original recipe's doc.
- **Source of truth stays the repo markdown**; each Doc is generated from it, so regenerating is always safe.

## One small call for you
With a doc-per-day, there's no single "whole week at a glance." Options:
- **(a)** the weekly *folder* is the index (docs named by day), or
- **(b)** I also drop a short **"Menu — Week of…" doc** at the top that lists each night → links to its recipe doc.

I lean **(b)** — it's the nicest phone landing page and still create-only. OK with that?

## The gating dependency (verify before Phase 3's recipe step)
This needs **Google OAuth** (Drive/Docs), and I need to confirm the connected Google tooling can actually **create a Doc with content** — the available Google MCP so far only showed *auth* tools, so it may be Drive-file-only, in which case we'd use the Docs API directly. This is the analog to our Apple-events test.

**Want me to kick off Google auth now and create a throwaway test doc** to prove the write path? You'll get an OAuth approval prompt in the browser. (Calendar/Reminders stay Apple + local — this only changes where recipes land.)

lastPrompt: google, new doc per recipe, so each day gets a new doc generated. That makes it easier to navigate.

a - weekly folder

yes try creating a test doc 
and here's a backup method that I want to explore as an option if needed https://github.com/a-bonus/google-docs-mcp
